stories.title: Monthly Property Transactions Statistics Released
stories.summary: HMRC published its monthly property transaction statistics for the UK. The next release covering June 2026 data is due 31 July 2026.

stories.title: Asylum Seekers Will Pay Towards Costs of Accommodation
stories.summary: The government announced plans requiring migrants to contribute around £10,000 towards the cost of their asylum support. The measure aims to reduce the burden on the public purse.

stories.title: New UK-France Deal on Small Boat Crossings Signed
stories.summary: The Home Secretary signed a landmark deal with France to boost enforcement action on beaches and put people smugglers behind bars, strengthening bilateral cooperation on illegal Channel crossings.

stories.title: New UK Ambassador to Turkey Appointed
stories.summary: Jennifer Elizabeth Anderson CMG has been appointed His Majesty's Ambassador to the Republic of Turkey, succeeding Jill Morris CMG.

stories.title: Youth Jobs Grant Launches Across Great Britain
stories.summary: The Youth Jobs Grant launched on 30 June 2026, offering a £3,000 grant to eligible employers who hire young people. Applications are open via the Find a Grant service.

stories.title: DWP Complaints Statistics to March 2026 Published
stories.summary: Official statistics covering DWP complaints received, closed, and upheld up to 31 March 2026 have been released, broken down by business area.

stories.title: Agriculture and Water Environment 2026 Report Published
stories.summary: DEFRA's 2026 report reveals 48% of non-permitted farms inspected between April 2021 and March 2026 were non-compliant with at least one environmental regulation, out of 19,018 inspections.

stories.title: £225m Capital Grants for Farmers and Land Managers
stories.summary: The Rural Payments Agency confirmed £225 million will be invested through Capital Grants this year to help farmers carry out environmental improvements across England.

stories.title: Sustainable Farming Incentive 2026 (SFI26) Guidance Published
stories.summary: New guidance released for farmers and land managers to prepare to apply for the Sustainable Farming Incentive 2026, which pays for sustainable land management activities that protect the environment.

stories.title: Defence Investment Plan: £298bn Over Four Years Announced
stories.summary: The government unveiled the Defence Investment Plan, backed by £298bn of investment over four years, raising annual defence spending from £54bn to nearly £80bn by 2029. It includes £3bn for space and £2.5bn for cyber capabilities.

stories.title: Red Arrows to Get New Jets as Part of £15bn Military Spending Boost
stories.summary: The RAF Red Arrows will receive new jets replacing their ageing Hawk aircraft as part of the £15bn increase in military spending announced in the Defence Investment Plan.

stories.title: Defence Secretary on 'Difficult but Necessary' Choices
stories.summary: Defence Secretary Dan Jarvis said the government made 'difficult but necessary' choices to fund the armed forces, with defence expenditure set to reach £80bn a year by 2029.

stories.title: New UK Steel Trade Measure Takes Effect from 1 July 2026
stories.summary: From today, the government is limiting tariff-free steel imports, reducing overall quota volumes by 51% compared with the previous steel safeguard measure. The policy was announced on 19 March 2026.

stories.title: Review of Post Office Unevidenced Shortfalls Process Published
stories.summary: An independent senior lawyers' review of the unevidenced shortfalls process under the Post Office Horizon compensation scheme has been published. The scheme, funded by DBT, closed for new applications on 31 January 2026.

agencies.name: DSIT
agencies.description: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

stories.title: UK-Germany Joint Statement on Advanced AI Safety and Security
stories.summary: The UK and Germany issued a joint statement committing to collaborate on ensuring advanced AI is developed safely, with risks rigorously understood and managed.

stories.title: Damning Amos Review into Maternity and Neonatal Care Published
stories.summary: The Amos review confirmed patients received unacceptable care in England's maternity and neonatal services, resulting in stillbirths, serious injuries, and maternal deaths. It calls for sweeping improvements.

stories.title: NHS Issues New PMOS Guidance for Women with Irregular Periods
stories.summary: NICE issued guidance recommending that women with irregular periods should be checked for polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome (PMOS), a condition described as underdiagnosed and inconsistently managed.

stories.title: Resident Doctors in England Accept Government Offer
stories.summary: Resident doctors in England accepted a government pay and conditions offer, resolving a long-running dispute that had led to repeated strike action across the NHS.

stories.title: Fairfax Avenue Post Office in Hull to Close
stories.summary: The Fairfax Avenue Post Office in Hull is set to close, described as a 'big loss' by local residents including those with mobility issues. Local MP Dame Diana Johnson has raised concerns about the impact on the community.
